title: microlicia ferruginea
intro: Microlicia ferruginea, also known as the rusty microlicia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is a shrub native to South America, particularly in Brazil, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. Microlicia ferruginea has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and inflammation, and treating wounds and infections. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture for use in herbal medicine, with the leaves and stems being the most commonly used parts.
